title: Convert case-with-nil in maybe_load_profile/1 into a function head
prompt: Refactor MyApp.UserService.maybe_load_profile/1 in test_fixtures/ruby_style_user_service.ex. The function calls `Repo.get_by/2` and then wraps the result in a `case nil -> nil; profile -> profile end`, which is an identity. Replace the whole body with a direct call — just return `Repo.get_by(MyApp.Profile, user_id: user.id)`. Rename the function to `load_profile/1` to reflect its actual contract (no `maybe_` prefix) and accept a destructured `%User{id: id}` in the head. Output a `defmodule MyApp.UserService do ... end` containing only the renamed function plus the necessary alias.
tier_rationale: Single-direction refactor; the tautological case block is an obvious removal. Renaming away from `maybe_` is a single textual change.
research_citation: te_chris, HN 46752907: 'maybe_do_blah helper functions everywhere. 30 lines when 8 would do.'
